subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: -0.71% | ⚠️ NVDA + AVGO both within 1% of stops
html_body:

$425.80$95.38+$8.39+9.64%⚠️ WATCH
NVDA Stop: $204.00 (just below $205 support level) | TP1: $218–225 (earnings gap / resistance) | TP2: $240
LLY Stop: $915.00 (well below) | TP1: $1,114 ✅ PASSED — at $1,133 | TP2: $1,243 | TP3: $1,393
AVGO Stop: $379.00 (post-earnings floor support) | TP1: $430–450 (gap recovery) | TP2: $481 (Jun 2 ATH)
TSM Stop: $357.00 (well below) | TP1: $435.00 — 2.1% away, prepare trim | TP2: $485
Prices: Fri Jun 12 close. Sources: FinanceCharts/Yahoo (NVDA, TSM), Morningstar (AVGO), CNBC/Investing.com (LLY). Cross-verified ≥2 sources each.
🚨 EXIT ALERTS
🚨 NVDA — 0.58% ABOVE STOP — CRITICAL
Current: $205.19  ·  Stop: $204.00  ·  Gap: $1.19 (0.58%)
200-day MA: ~$214 (Investing.com) — stock is below its 200-day MA. Bearish signal.
Support: $205.09 per ChartMill. Stop at $204 is correctly placed just below this.
Earnings (May 20): Beat hard — $81.6B revenue (+85% YoY), EPS $1.87 vs $1.76 est. CEO: Blackwell sold out. Market reaction: −1.77%. Classic sell-the-news. Fundamentals intact, technicals broken.
This morning matters. Any weakness in the S&P open and NVDA tests $204. Set a stop-market order in your broker tonight before sleep. If stop hits:
Net exit: 1.3799 × $204 − $3 commission = $278.50 vs $300.06 cost = −$21.56 loss
🚨 AVGO — 0.81% ABOVE STOP — CLOSEST TO EDGE
Current: $382.07  ·  Stop: $379.00  ·  Gap: $3.07 (0.81%)
Earnings context (Jun 3): Revenue +48% YoY, AI +143%, EPS beat ($2.44 vs $2.40). Q3 guide $29.4B, AI crossing $16B. Strong. BUT market expected full-year $100B AI target to be raised — Hock Tan didn't raise it. −14% day after earnings.
Support: $375–380 is the post-earnings floor. Stop at $379 = bottom of the consolidation. If $379 breaks, thesis isn't broken but the range is done.
Thesis check: AI semi fundamentals intact (AI revenue +143%). This is an expectations-reset selloff, not a business deterioration.
If both NVDA and AVGO stop out: Net loss −$34.96 combined. Portfolio value ≈ $815 (still on path to $1,000 goal).
Net exit at stop: 0.315 × $379 − $3 = $116.39 vs $129.79 cost = −$13.40 loss
🔔 LLY — ABOVE TP1 $1,114 — HOLD (trim math fails at this size)
Current: $1,133.00  ·  TP1 passed: $1,114 ✅  ·  TP2 target: $1,243
Trim math (30% = 0.02958 shares at $1,133):
Gross proceeds: $33.53 · Cost basis sold: $29.43 · Gross profit: +$4.10
Exit commission: −$3.00 · Net profit: +$1.10
Rule check: Gross profit $4.10 > $3 commission ✓ (threshold cleared). But $1.10 net is trivial and the freed $30 is too small for any meaningful redeployment (min position size rule violated).
Recommendation: HOLD. Wait for LLY toward $1,243 (TP2) — at that level: 30% trim nets ($1,243−$994.87)×0.02958 = $7.34 gross −$3 = +$4.34 net. Wait for it.
Friday pullback: LLY dropped −2.41% on Fri from $1,161 open to $1,133. GLP-1 thesis intact. Orforglipron oral launch in progress. Hold.
⚠️ TSM — APPROACHING TP1 $435 — 2.1% AWAY — PREPARE TRIM
Current: $425.80  ·  TP1: $435 (2.1% away)  ·  TP2: $485
TP1 trim math (50% = 0.112 shares at $435):
Gross: $48.72 · Cost basis sold: $43.49 · Gross profit: +$5.23 · Commission: −$3.00 · Net: +$2.23 ✓
Action: Place a limit sell order for 0.112 shares at $435.00. Frees $45.72 net cash.
Remaining position after trim: 0.112 shares × $435 = $48.72 (still meaningful).
What to do with freed cash: Hold as dry powder or deploy into VERA/VRDN once IBKR approves.
Thesis: AI foundry demand strongest holding in portfolio. TSMC is capacity-constrained through 2027. No fundamental concerns. TSM is the clean position right now.
⚡ Position Risk Scores
TickerScoreLabelDriver
NVDA52/100HIGHBelow 200-day MA, near stop, post-earnings drift
AVGO48/100MOD-HIGHExpectations miss, post-earnings floor fragile
LLY34/100MODERATEPullback from ATH, GLP-1 thesis intact
TSM35/100MODERATEStrongest position, geopolitical tail the only concern
⏳ Pending Watchlist — Waiting on IBKR Approval
Both positions IBKR-blocked. Clocks ticking on both PDUFAs.
VERA Vera TherapeuticsBUY NOW 7/10
🔒 IBKR application pending — current broker does not allow this stock
Price: ~$34.26 vs entry target $34.04 → ✅ +0.6% — entry still valid
Stop: $24.50  |  TP1: $55.00 (sell 50%)  |  TP2: $78.00 (sell 50%)
🟢 22 DAYS TO PDUFA Atacicept (BAFF/APRIL) · IgA Nephropathy · July 7, 2026
Approval probability: 89%
BTD + Priority Review + p<0.0001 Phase 3 + FDA eGFR alignment + $5.28M director open-market buy. Zero CRLs ever issued for an IgAN PDUFA — every one has been approved. $714M cash. Analyst avg target $78 (14 analysts, all Strong Buy). Q3 eGFR data = second catalyst leg.
Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — pre-revenue biotech, check debt structure on Musaffa before entry.
VERA is AT your entry price right now. 22 days to PDUFA. Get IBKR approval done this week. When approved: enter $240 at ~$34, stop $24.50. Risk: $9.76/share (28.5%). Upside to TP1 $55: +$20.74/share (60.5%). EV on $240 position (89% approval base): +$108 net.
VRDN Viridian TherapeuticsWATCH 6/10
🔒 IBKR application pending — current broker does not allow this stock
Price: ~$15.73 (Jun 8 ref) vs entry target $20.00 → ⚠️ Price dropped −21.4% below target — verify thesis
Stop: $14.50  |  TP1: $28.00  |  TP2: $37.00
⚠️ Price drift: VRDN fell from $20 target to ~$15.73 (−21.4%). But this is actually better entry risk: entry $15.73 + stop $14.50 = only $1.23 risk per share (7.8%) vs original $20 entry. Selloff was post-REVEAL-1 data for subcutaneous elegrobart (separate program) — veligrotug PDUFA thesis is unchanged. Run analyze VRDN to re-derive fresh levels before entering.
🟢 15 DAYS TO PDUFA Veligrotug (IV IGF-1R) · Thyroid Eye Disease · June 30, 2026
Approval probability: 82%
$762M cash. Commercial launch infrastructure ready (field team hired). Priority Review. TED market significant. Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — interest income vs milestone ratio unclear, check Musaffa.
Limitation: float $2.9B (large), SI 8.56% (not squeeze), no specialist biotech insider buying. Conviction limited.
🚨 Only 15 days to PDUFA — if IBKR not approved in time, this entry window closes. If price dips further, R:R improves. If IBKR approves this week, verify Musaffa first, then enter near current price ($15-16) rather than original $20 target.
📡 Catalyst Plays — June 15, 2026
15-search live screen. 2 catalyst finds + 1 Tier-A fallback (no 3rd catalyst passed all gates).
UNCY Unicycive Therapeutics — Oxylanthanum Carbonate (OLC)👁 WATCH
Catalyst: NDA PDUFA (CRL Class II resubmission) — June 27, 2026 — 12 days away
Market cap: ~$154M · Price: ~$7.60 (Jun 1) · Short interest: 8.56% (4.2 days to cover)
Indication: Hyperphosphatemia in CKD dialysis patients (phosphate binder). Niche indication, no major competitive blockade in resubmission path.
Probability: ~70% — CRL Class II resubmission historical approval rate.
Bull: Approval → $12–15 (+60–100%)  Bear: Second CRL → $3–4 (−50–60%)
Cash: $57M+ (Q1 loss $12.8M) — ~4 quarters runway. No dilution imminent.
Smart money: No insider buys or institutional buildup visible.
Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — phosphate binder likely clean (no intoxicant concern), but pre-revenue structure needs Musaffa check.
Approval probability: 70%
Conviction: 3/5 → WATCH. Small cap, 12 days to event, but limited smart money signal and no squeeze setup (SI only 8.56%). EV positive if thesis holds. Say analyze UNCY for full deep dive.
ALLO Allogene Therapeutics — ALLO-329 (CAR-T Autoimmune)⛔ SKIP
Catalyst: Phase 1 ALLO-329 data readout — June 2026 (no confirmed exact date)
Market cap: ~$505M · Price: ~$1.88 (Jun 5) · Short interest: 26.22% (high, squeeze-eligible)
Reason to skip: No confirmed exact event date (Gate 1 borderline). Phase 1 readout = safety/dosing data, rarely produces squeeze-level moves. Stock at $1.88 signals heavy dilution. M&A speculation (BMS/Gilead active in CAR-T) could change the story — but that's not a confirmed catalyst. Halal unverified. If Phase 1 data drops positive and stock gaps up with 26% SI, monitor for a Phase 2 catalyst trade later.
Conviction: 2/5 → SKIP.
ASML ASML Holding — EUV Monopoly (Tier A Fallback)📦 FALLBACK
⚠️ No 3rd near-term catalyst found — fallback from Tier A rotation playbook
Not a binary catalyst play. ASML makes the EUV machines that TSMC (which you own) needs to manufacture chips. Every next-gen node TSMC adds = more ASML machines ordered. With $725B in 2026 hyperscaler capex, the TSMC expansion is direct ASML revenue. Halal verified (AAOIFI). No weekly catalyst, but structurally the strongest AI infrastructure name outside your existing TSM/NVDA.
Relevant only if: stop-outs free significant cash and you want AI exposure with less single-name risk than NVDA.
💵 Cash Deployment — $213.81 available
After $3 reserve + $3 buy commission: $207.81 deployable. Large enough for any of the watchlist positions.

Recommendation: Hold. Do not deploy this week.

With NVDA 0.58% above its stop and AVGO 0.81% above its stop, this cash is your buffer. Deploying into a new position while two existing ones are teetering on stops adds risk in the wrong direction. Let Monday's open resolve the stop situations first.

If both stops hit: Cash grows to ~$608. Portfolio becomes LLY + TSM + $608 cash = ~$815 total. From there you have real firepower to enter VERA + VRDN when IBKR approves (combined target $480 across both).

Your call.
🔄 Rotations: None suggested this session. Anti-rotation rule active — two positions within 2% of their stops. Let the stops work. Revisit after Monday open resolves NVDA and AVGO.
📚 Concept #19 — Sector Rotation (XLK / XLV / XLF)
Look at your own portfolio right now: NVDA is −5.6%, AVGO is −7.3%, TSM is +9.6%, LLY is +13.9%. Same four names you bought in May — but the AI semis are bleeding while pharma is printing. That's not random. That's sector rotation — institutional money moving from XLK (tech ETF) into XLV (healthcare ETF). Money flows between sectors constantly, and the sector ETFs let you measure it in real time: XLK/SPY trending down = tech underperforming; XLV/SPY breaking out = healthcare has the flow. Over the past few weeks, NVDA and AVGO have been in sell-the-news mode post-earnings while LLY and NVO have been near multi-week highs. That divergence in your own book is textbook rotation. The highest-level trade in equities isn't which stock to pick — it's which sector has the institutional bid. Watch XLK vs XLV on a weekly chart. When XLK crosses back above XLV relative performance, that's the signal the AI trade is resuming. You're already positioned on both sides of this rotation — that's the diversification working.
Also today: Defensive vs cyclical sectors — defensives (XLV, XLP staples, XLU utilities) outperform when growth is slowing; cyclicals (XLF financials, XLY consumer discretionary, XLI industrials) outperform in expansion. The rotation between them is the single biggest portfolio driver across any 6-month window.
Prices as of Friday June 12, 2026 close. Sources: FinanceCharts / Yahoo Finance (NVDA $205.19, TSM $425.80), Morningstar / CNBC (AVGO $382.07), Investing.com / CNBC (LLY $1,133.00). Watchlist: VRDN ~$15.73 (Jun 8 Tickeron/Morningstar), VERA ~$34.26 (TradingView Jun 12–14). Catalyst screening via MarketBeat, StockTitan, SEC EDGAR, BiopharmaWatch. All prices cross-verified ≥2 sources. Portfolio last updated: June 9, 2026.

This is research and information only, not financial advice. All investment and trading decisions are yours alone. Halal compliance must be verified via Musaffa / AAOIFI before entering any new position. Broker commission: $3 per trade (buy or sell); entry commissions already paid on all open positions — only exit commissions remain.
